briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to replace 1965 Zoning Bylaw with new 2026 version
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposal to repeal Burnaby's old Zoning Bylaw from 1965 and replace it with a new Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 2026. This proposed change would also introduce new Development Permit Area Guidelines and reassign some properties to different zoning districts. If approved, this would update the rules for what can be built and where, affecting many local businesses and property owners.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the public hearing date — this is your chance to comment on how the new rules could affect your property or business.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail
briefs.source_body:
7.1
BURNABY ZONING BYLAW - PROPOSED REPEAL AND REPLACEMENT
Purpose: To seek Council approval to repeal Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965 and replace it with the proposed Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 2026, including new Development Permit Area Guidelines and zoning district reassignment.

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Housing Authority Year 2 operating funding request
briefs.summary: Council will consider approving the Burnaby Housing Authority's request for Year 2 operating funding. This is a proposal to provide money to help the Authority run its housing programs for another year. The decision has not been made yet — Council will vote on it at an upcoming meeting.
briefs.source_body:
7.2
BHA - ASSISTANCE REQUEST FOR YEAR 2 OPERATING FUNDING
Purpose: To seek Council approval of the Burnaby Housing Authority Corporation’s assistance request for Year 2 Operating Funding.

briefs.title: Proposed: 2026 property tax rates for Burnaby businesses
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on approving the 2026 property tax rates for municipal, regional, and Business Improvement Area (BIA) taxes. A BIA is a local area where businesses pay extra taxes to fund improvements like street cleaning, marketing, or events. If approved, these rates will determine how much property tax your business pays in 2026. This is a proposed decision — it has not been finalized yet.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, these rates will affect your 2026 property tax bill.
briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail, Food Service
briefs.source_body:
7.3
2026 PROPERTY TAX RATES
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for 2026 municipal, regional and business improvement area (BIA) property tax rates.

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to appoint an Approving Officer
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposal to appoint an Approving Officer. This officer is a person who has the authority to approve or reject applications for things like subdivision plans (dividing land into smaller lots) and other land-related approvals. The appointment is a routine administrative step, but it affects how land development applications are processed in Burnaby. The decision has not been made yet — Council will consider and vote on the appointment at an upcoming meeting.
briefs.action_required: No action required from you at this time. Watch for the council decision — this could affect how quickly land development applications are processed.
briefs.source_body:
7.4
APPOINTMENT OF APPROVING OFFICER
Purpose: To seek Council approval to appoint an Approving Officer.

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby council to vote on retaining wall height variance at 8012 11th Ave
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a Development Variance Permit (DVP) for a property at 8012 11th Avenue. A DVP is a special permission that lets a property owner break certain rules in the city's zoning bylaw. In this case, the owner wants permission to build retaining walls that are taller than what the current rules allow. The decision has not been made yet — council will discuss and vote on this proposal at an upcoming meeting.
briefs.action_required: If you own or are developing property near 8012 11th Avenue, watch for the council decision — it could set a precedent for similar height variance requests in your area.
briefs.source_body:
7.5
DVP #26-01 - 8012 11TH AVENUE - RETAINING WALLS HEIGHT VARIANCES
Purpose: To seek Council approval for Development Variance Permit (DVP) #26-01.

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby council to vote on insurance contract renewals
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on awarding contracts for the city's annual property and liability insurance programs. This is a routine renewal of insurance coverage that protects city assets and operations. The decision is up for approval at an upcoming council meeting — it has not been finalized yet.
briefs.source_body:
7.6
2026 INSURANCE PROGRAMS - CONTRACT RENEWALS
Purpose: To request approval to award insurance contracts for the annual property and liability insurance programs.

briefs.title: Proposed: Council to approve Heritage Commission members attending 2026 BC conference
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposal to allow members of Burnaby's Community Heritage Commission (CHC) to attend the 2026 Annual Heritage BC Conference in Squamish, BC, from May 4-6, 2026. The CHC advises Council on heritage matters, and this trip would let members learn from other communities and bring back ideas. The decision has not been made yet — Council will consider whether to approve the travel and related costs at an upcoming meeting.

key_dates.label: Heritage BC Conference in Squamish — proposed attendance dates
Heritage BC Conference in Squamish — proposed attendance dates

briefs.source_body:
7.7
2026 HERITAGE BC CONFERENCE
Purpose: To seek Council approval for members of the Community Heritage Commission (CHC) to attend the 2026 Annual Heritage BC Conference in Squamish, BC from May 4-6, 2026.

briefs.title: Council to vote on contract award for networking software licenses
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed contract award for the supply and delivery of networking software licenses. If approved, this would authorize the city to purchase the software needed to run its computer networks. This is a routine procurement decision that keeps city technology systems working.
briefs.source_body:
7.8
CA - NETWORKING SOFTWARE LICENSES
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the supply and delivery of networking software licenses.

briefs.title: Council to vote on contract for new civic facility furniture
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed contract award for the supply, delivery, and installation of office and facility furniture for the new civic facility. If approved, this contract would cover all the furniture and equipment needed to fit out the new building. The decision is on the agenda for the upcoming council meeting and has not yet been finalized.
briefs.source_body:
7.9
CA - NEW CIVIC FACILITY FIT-OUT, FURNISHINGS AND EQUIPMENT
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the supply, delivery and installation of office and facility furniture.

briefs.title: Council to vote on contract change for external audit services
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed contract change for the city's external audit services. This decision would approve a change to the existing contract with the company that audits the city's financial statements. If approved, the change would take effect for the upcoming fiscal year. The specific details of the contract change, such as the new cost or scope of work, are not provided in the agenda item.
briefs.source_body:
7.10
CC - EXTERNAL AUDIT SERVICES
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Change (CC) for External Audit Services.

briefs.title: Proposed: Contract change for geotechnical work at Burnaby Lake Recreation Complex
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed contract change (a change to an existing agreement) for geotechnical consulting services at the Burnaby Lake Recreation Complex. Geotechnical consultants study soil and ground conditions to make sure buildings are safe and stable. This change would increase the amount paid to the consultant for extra work on the project. The decision has not been made yet — council will discuss and vote on it at the upcoming meeting.
briefs.action_required: No action needed from you — this is an internal city contract decision. Watch for the council vote if you are a contractor or supplier to the project.
briefs.source_body:
7.11
CC - BURNABY LAKE RECREATION COMPLEX GEOTECHNICAL CONSULTING SERVICES
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Change (CC) for geotechnical consultant services for the Burnaby Lake Recreation Complex project.

briefs.title: Council to vote on Spring 2026 community grant recipients
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on approving the recommended recipients for the Spring 2026 community grant intake. This decision will determine which local organizations and projects receive funding from the city. The grants are part of the city's regular program to support community groups, and council will decide whether to accept the staff recommendations.
briefs.source_body:
8.1
ECC - SPRING 2026 COMMUNITY GRANT APPLICATIONS
Purpose: To seek Council approval for the recommended grant recipients for the Spring 2026 intake.

briefs.title: Proposed: Mid-year 2026 fee changes to Burnaby Consolidated Fees Bylaw
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on proposed mid-year 2026 amendments to the Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw. If approved, this would change certain fees that businesses and residents pay for city services, permits, and licences. The exact fees affected have not been detailed yet, but the proposal signals that some costs could go up or down later in 2026. Business owners should watch for the specific fee schedules that will be amended.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision and the updated fee schedule — this could affect your business licence or permit costs.
briefs.source_body:
8.2
FMC - BURNABY CONSOLIDATED FEES AND CHARGES BYLAW - 2026 MID-YEAR AMENDMENTS
Purpose: To propose mid-year 2026 fee amendments to certain fee schedules within the Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw.

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Tenant Protection Bylaw up for council vote
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed Tenant Protection Bylaw and framework. If approved, this bylaw would create new rules for landlords, such as requiring a specific reason to end a tenancy and giving tenants more notice. The goal is to protect renters from unfair evictions and rent increases. Council will consider endorsing the framework and bylaw at an upcoming meeting.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect your rental property operations if you are a landlord in Burnaby.
briefs.source_body:
8.3
PDC - PROPOSED TENANT PROTECTION FRAMEWORK AND TENANT PROTECTION BYLAW AND ASSOCIATED BYLAW AMENDMENTS
Purpose: To seek Council endorsement of the proposed tenant protection framework and Burnaby Tenant Protection Bylaw and associated bylaw amendments.

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to vote on Southeast Bikeway upgrades
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on proposed improvements for the Southeast Bikeway Upgrade Project. If approved, this project would add new bike lanes, crossings, and safety features along the route. The decision is not final yet — Council will discuss and vote on the plan at an upcoming meeting. Business owners near the bikeway should watch for the outcome, as construction could affect local traffic and parking.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the Council decision — if approved, prepare for possible construction impacts on your business access and parking.
briefs.source_body:
8.4
TC - SOUTHEAST BIKEWAY UPGRADE PROJECT - PROPOSED IMPROVEMENTS
Purpose: To seek Council approval of the proposed improvements as part of the Southeast Bikeway Upgrade Project.

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to update design rules for safer intersections
briefs.summary: Council will consider a proposal to update the Burnaby Town Centre Design Standards. The change would require new 'protected intersection' designs that make crossings safer for people walking and biking. This is not a final decision yet — Council is set to vote on whether to move forward with the amendment. If approved, the new rules would apply to future development and road projects in the Town Centre area.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote — if approved, this could affect your development or renovation plans in Burnaby's Town Centre.
briefs.source_body:
8.5
TC - PROPOSED AMENDMENT TO THE BURNABY TOWN CENTRE DESIGN STANDARDS TO INCORPORATE PROTECTED INTERSECTION GEOMETRY
Purpose: To seek Council endorsement to amend the Burnaby Town Centre Design Standards to incorporate protected intersection geometry, improve road safety for pedestrians and cyclists and support all-ages-and-abilities mobility.

briefs.title: Council to receive March 2026 building permit activity report
briefs.summary: Council will consider a report on building permits issued in Burnaby from March 1 to March 31, 2026. This report gives council information on construction activity in the city, including the number and value of permits issued. The report is for information only — no bylaw changes or new rules are proposed at this time. However, the data may signal trends that could affect future development or permit fees.
briefs.source_body:
9.2
SU: BUILDING PERMIT ACTIVITY REPORT NO. 03 FROM MARCH 1 - MARCH 31, 2026
Purpose: To provide Council with information on construction activity as reflected by the building permits that have been issued for the subject period.

briefs.title: Proposed: Heritage designation for 3789 Royal Oak Avenue
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council will consider a report that recommends adding the property at 3789 Royal Oak Avenue to the city's heritage register. The report includes an 'as-found' condition report and a 'Statement of Significance' — a document that explains why the building is historically important. This is a first step. If Council later votes to formally designate the property, the owner would need approval from the city before making major changes to the building's exterior. No decision has been made yet.
briefs.action_required: If you own this property, watch for a public hearing notice — you may want to attend or submit comments before Council votes.
briefs.source_body:
9.3
CHC - AS-FOUND REPORT AND STATEMENT OF SIGNIFICANCE FOR HERITAGE RESOURCES AT 3789 ROYAL OAK AVENUE
Purpose: To provide Council an as-found report and Statement of Significance for heritage resources located at 7389 Royal Oak Avenue for information.

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby solid waste and recycling 2025 annual report
briefs.summary: Council will consider the City of Burnaby's 2025 annual report on solid waste and recycling collection. This report summarizes how the city's garbage, recycling, and organics collection system performed over the past year. It is a review of current operations, not a proposed bylaw or new rule. No changes to fees, services, or regulations are being voted on at this time.
briefs.industries: Retail, Food Service, Landlord & Property Mgmt
briefs.source_body:
9.4
EC - SOLID WASTE AND RECYCLING (SWR) 2025 ANNUAL REPORT
Purpose: To provide Council with a summary of the City of Burnaby solid waste and recycling collection system performance.

briefs.title: Council to receive update on proposed food sustainability measures in Burnaby
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to receive an operational update on proposed food sustainability measures. This update will outline current and planned actions the City is considering to improve local food systems, reduce food waste, and increase access to healthy food. No decisions have been made yet — this is a report to inform Council before any future votes on specific bylaws or programs.
briefs.industries: Food Service, Grocery & Food Retail, Agriculture
briefs.source_body:
9.5
EC - SU: ENHANCING FOOD SUSTAINABILITY IN BURNABY
Purpose: To provide Council with an operational update on food sustainability measures within the City of Burnaby.

briefs.title: Proposed: Safety upgrades at Central Boulevard and Metropolis parkade intersection
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to consider proposed safety improvements at the intersection of Central Boulevard and the Metropolis at Metrotown underground parkade access. The proposal aims to address safety concerns at this busy intersection. A decision has not yet been made — Council will review the plan at an upcoming meeting.
briefs.source_body:
9.6
TC - CENTRAL BOULEVARD AND PARKADE INERSECTION SAFETY IMPROVEMENTS
Purpose: To provide Council with an overview of the proposed safety improvements at the intersection of Central Boulevard and Metropolis at Metrotown underground parkade access.

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby council to vote on rezoning for hotel, more homes at 3900 Grand Promenade
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would change the zoning for four properties near the Carrigan area. The developer wants to replace some planned office space with a hotel and restaurant, and add 362 more market strata (condo) units. This would make two planned towers taller — one by seven storeys, the other by four. The developer also wants to move the required affordable housing for this first phase to a different site (the Carrigan site), instead of building it here. This is an amendment to a rezoning that was already approved in 2024.
briefs.action_required: Attend the public hearing or submit a written comment to Burnaby City Council before the vote.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Hotel & Accommodation, Landlord & Property Mgmt
briefs.source_body:
11.1.1
#14808 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 7, 2026 - REZ #24-23 (3900 Grand Promenade, 6950 Austin Rd., 9858 & 9898 Gatineau Place)
Purpose: to amend the previous rezoning of the subject lands pursuant to REZ #18-32 (Bylaw No. 14447), which received Final Adoption on September 23, 2024, and included two market strata residential buildings of 80 (Tower 1B) and 73 (Tower 1A) storeys, respectively, inclusive of a 13-storey retail and office podium. As part of the subject amendment rezoning application, the applicant is proposing to replace a portion of the commercial office use with hotel and restaurant uses, and add 362 additional market strata units from the development concept approved under REZ #18-32, resulting in an additional seven storeys for Tower 1B and four storeys for Tower 1A. In addition, as part of the subject amendment rezoning application, the applicant is proposing to provide the inclusionary requirement associated with Phase 1 only, to be transferred to the Carrigan site, which is an amendment from the previous rezoning approval.

briefs.title: Proposed: 12-storey mixed-use development at 6535 Kingsway
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw change that would rezone 6535 Kingsway. If approved, the change would allow a developer to build a 12-storey building with 290 residential units, ground-floor shops facing Kingsway, and underground parking. The proposal matches the city's Official Community Plan (OCP) — the long-term vision for how the city should grow — and the Edmonds Community Plan. This is not yet approved; Council will consider it at an upcoming meeting.
briefs.action_required: Attend the public hearing or submit written comments to Council before the vote to share your support or concerns.
briefs.source_body:
11.1.2
#14809 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 8, 2026 - REZ #25-09 (6535 Kingsway)
Purpose: to permit the construction of a 12-storey mixed-use multiple-family development with a double-height commercial storey at street level comprised of 290 residential units, ground-oriented commercial uses fronting Kingsway, and underground parking. The proposal is consistent with the applicable OCP and the Edmonds Community Plan designations for the site.

briefs.title: Proposed: Five-storey self-storage building at 2757 Norland Avenue
briefs.summary: Council will consider a proposed bylaw change to rezone 2757 Norland Avenue. If approved, the change would allow a five-storey enclosed self-storage building to be built on the site. The proposal follows the city's 1998 Official Community Plan and special transition rules. This is not yet approved — it is up for a council vote.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, this could affect nearby property values and traffic.
briefs.source_body:
11.1.3
#14810 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 9, 2026 - REZ #25-21 (2757 Norland Avenue)
Purpose: to permit the construction of a five-storey enclosed self-storage building. The proposal is being advanced in accordance with the applicable 1998 OCP policies and approved transitionary provisions.

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby e-bike and e-scooter pilot bylaw amendment
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed amendment to Burnaby's Street and Traffic Bylaw that would create a pilot program for e-bikes and electric kick scooters. If approved, this would change the rules for where and how these devices can be used on city streets. The decision has not been made yet — the amendment is up for discussion and a vote at an upcoming council meeting.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, new rules for e-bikes and e-scooters could affect your business operations or delivery services.
briefs.source_body:
11.2.1
#14811 - Burnaby Street and Traffic Bylaw 1961, Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026
Purpose: to amend Burnaby Street and Traffic Bylaw 1964 (e-bike and electric kick scooter pilot program)

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby e-bike and e-scooter pilot in parks
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would allow e-bikes and electric kick scooters in certain Burnaby parks as part of a pilot program. The amendment to the Parks Regulation Bylaw 1979 would create new rules for where and how these electric devices can be used. This is not yet law — it is up for approval at a future council meeting.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, this pilot could affect where you can ride e-bikes or e-scooters in Burnaby parks.
briefs.source_body:
11.2.2
#14812 - Burnaby Parks Regulation Bylaw 1979, Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026
Purpose: to amend the Burnaby Parks Regulation Bylaw 1979 (e-bike and electric kick scooter pilot program)

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby bylaw amendment for e-bike and e-scooter pilot enforcement
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would update how the city enforces rules for e-bikes and electric kick scooters during a pilot program. The amendment to the Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009 would allow the city to issue tickets and fines for violations related to these devices. This is not yet law — it is up for approval at a future council meeting.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, new enforcement rules for e-bikes and e-scooters could affect your business operations.
briefs.source_body:
11.2.3
#14813 - Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009, Amendment Bylaw No. 3, 2026 (e-bike and electric kick scooter programs)
Purpose: to amend the Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009 (e-bike and electric kick scooter pilot program)

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby 2026 property tax rates for all land and buildings
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would set the 2026 tax rates for all taxable land and buildings in Burnaby. This bylaw, called the Burnaby Rates Bylaw 2026, is required under sections 197 and 216 of the Community Charter (the main law that gives cities their powers). If approved, it will determine how much property tax you pay next year based on the assessed value of your land and any improvements (like buildings or renovations). This is a routine annual decision, but the specific rates could affect your business costs.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — the new rates will directly affect your 2026 property tax bill.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Rates Bylaw 2026 — council vote on proposed tax rates

briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail, Professional Services

Purpose: to establish tax rates upon all taxable land and improvements pursuant to sections 197 and 216 of the Community Charter
